conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know that you have Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with you in the UK.

To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask: Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you provide for Alex's care and upbringing (for example, providing food, clothing, shelter, and looking after their general welfare)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm fully responsible for Alex. I provide everything - food, clothing, a home, and I look after all of their needs and general welfare.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming that.

Next, I need to ask: Are you or Alex currently living in the UK? And do you (or your partner if you have one) have the right to claim benefits in the UK - for example, are you a British citizen, have settled status, or have another immigration status that allows you to claim benefits?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK. I'm in England - we live in Manchester. And yes, I have the right to claim benefits here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

Now I need to ask: Is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex, such as Alex's other parent or another person who Alex lives with?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's no one else claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me - Alex lives with me and nobody else is getting Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for clarifying that.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

1. Is Alex in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? (Given that Alex is 8 years old, I expect they're in primary school, but I need to confirm their education status.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in primary school - just regular primary school for their age.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One final question: Do you or your partner (if you have one) have individual income over £60,000 per year? This is important because if either of you earn over £50,000, there may be a High Income Child Benefit Charge that applies.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You are responsible for Alex's care and welfare
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You have the right to claim benefits in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is in full-time education (primary school)
- Your household income is below the threshold that would trigger the High Income Child Benefit Charge

You meet all the necessary criteria to claim Child Benefit for Alex. You can apply through the government's official channels by filling out the Child Benefit claim form. You'll need to provide Alex's birth certificate as part of your application.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education/training), 2) Alex lives in the UK with the claimant, 3) The claimant is responsible for Alex, providing for their care, food, clothing, shelter and general welfare, 4) The claimant has the right to claim benefits in the UK, 5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, 6) Alex is in full-time education (primary school) as appropriate for their age, 7) Neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, so there would be no High Income Child Benefit Charge concerns.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 8), who lives with them in Manchester, UK. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and welfare, has the right to claim benefits in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is in primary school education as appropriate for their age. The claimant's household income is under the High Income Child Benefit Charge threshold. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 8), who lives with them in Manchester, UK. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and welfare, has the right to claim benefits in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is in primary school education as appropriate for their age. The claimant's household income is under the High Income Child Benefit Charge threshold. All eligibility criteria are met.
